title: 25 Essential Thought Leaders in Semiconductors
text:
Daniel Nenni
Founder
Staff member

Why Semiconductor Thought Leadership Matters Right Now​
The semiconductor industry is not just another sector of the global economy. Approximately 1.05 trillion chips were sold globally in 2025 at an average price of US$0.74 per chip, according to Deloitte's 2026 outlook. Building a single advanced fabrication facility now costs approximately US$19 billion, and designing each new chip costs approximately US$1 billion on average. The decisions made in research labs, boardrooms, and government ministries about who makes chips, where, using what technology, and for whom will determine competitive advantage, national security, and economic resilience for decades.

The KPMG 2026 Global Semiconductor Industry Confidence Index reached 63, the third highest in two decades, driven by AI demand. But for the first time in the survey's history, industry leaders now rank tariffs and trade policy as their top concern, above talent shortages and competition. The semiconductor industry is simultaneously a technology story, a geopolitics story, a supply chain story, a sustainability story, and a workforce story. The voices on this list understand all those dimensions at once and share what they know in ways that move the conversation forward.

7. Daniel Nenni​Daniel Nenni is the Founder and Principal of SemiWiki, the open forum for semiconductor professionals that has become one of the most active practitioner communities in the industry. With more than four decades of experience in the global semiconductor industry, including senior roles at Intel, he co-authored Fabless: The Transformation of the Semiconductor Industry and Mobile Unleashed, a history of the ARM processor ecosystem. His LinkedIn network has reached the platform's 30,000 connection limit, reflecting the density of his relationships across every layer of the semiconductor industry.

His contribution is his stewardship of a practitioner community where working semiconductor professionals share genuine technical insights rather than promotional content. SemiWiki covers EDA, process technology, IP, and supply chain, and its editorial perspective shaped by Nenni's 40-plus years in the industry gives it a credibility that purely news-driven platforms cannot match.

25 Essential Thought Leaders in Semiconductors

Last updated: June 2026Semiconductors power every significant technology on earth. The chips inside AI servers, electric vehicles, smartphones, medical devices, and defence systems share one common thread: the people who understand how they are designed, made, traded, governed, and written about...
